# Phase 10.0.12 Self-Correction Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, autonomous constitutional self-rewrite systems, recursive governance mutation systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve historical reflection, failure analysis, institutional humility, self-audit continuity, and constitutional self-correction after governance literacy and knowledge-transfer doctrine.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional self-correction doctrine.
- Governance failure-analysis philosophy.
- Institutional humility survivability standards.
- Civilization historical-reflection architecture.
- Constitutional mistake-preservation doctrine.
- Governance learning-from-failure framework.
- Institutional self-audit continuity philosophy.
- Civilization correction-without-instability doctrine.
- Constitutional retrospective survivability standards.
- Governance error-containment doctrine.
- Phase 10.0.12 self-correction summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No self-correction layer creates runtime authority.
- No failure-analysis process bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No reflection layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No correction framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Self-correction remains human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, and non-self-rewriting.
- Retrospectives preserve original records and correction notes.
- Failure analysis preserves humility, dissent, context, and prohibited authority.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, autonomous constitutional self-rewrite systems, recursive governance mutation systems, or execution systems.
